Across Aotearoa New Zealand, AI is furthest along in supervised, lower-risk operational uses—especially in healthcare, education and agriculture—while high-stakes autonomous decision-making remains less mature, constrained by governance, privacy, trust, capability and infrastructure requirements.

This living whitepaper currently tracks AI in academic research, agriculture, creative industries, education, finance, healthcare, and the public sector in Aotearoa New Zealand.

Updated 11 March 2026
